cmake_minimum_required(VERSION 3.27)

# ${PROJECT_NAME} — Chronon3D project (generated by `chronon create`).
# Project name is derived from the directory name; override with:
#   cmake -B build -DPROJECT_NAME=my-video
project(${PROJECT_NAME}
    VERSION 0.0.1
    LANGUAGES CXX
    DESCRIPTION "Chronon3D project created with 'chronon create'"
)

# Use the canonical Chronon3D SDK (installed via `cmake --install` or
# available system-wide).  The user MUST have Chronon3D installed
# (see https://chronon3d.com/install) before running `cmake -B build`.
find_package(Chronon3D CONFIG REQUIRED)

# Pre-condition: the documented public API target must be intact.
# If the install script ever strips this target, this fails fast with a
# clear error instead of producing a vague link error further downstream.
if(NOT TARGET Chronon3D::SDK)
    message(FATAL_ERROR
        "Install boundary broken: Chronon3D::SDK target absent. "
        "Install Chronon3D first (https://chronon3d.com/install).")
endif()

# Build the standalone binary.  It registers the "HelloWorld" composition
# via src/hello_world.cpp and renders it directly via sdk::RenderEngine.
add_executable(${PROJECT_NAME} src/root.cpp src/hello_world.cpp)
set_target_properties(${PROJECT_NAME} PROPERTIES
    CXX_STANDARD 20
    CXX_STANDARD_REQUIRED ON
)

# Single PUBLIC-API link call (chronon3d_sdk_impl propagates transitively
# through Chronon3D::SDK at link time, per V0.1 SDK manifest).
target_link_libraries(${PROJECT_NAME} PRIVATE Chronon3D::SDK)
